mappings.notes: Store-data correctness: when a store is requested and granted, the data written to memory (data_wdata_o) must match the data input (lsu_wdata_i) aligned to the byte offset indicated by the lower 2 bits of the address. This captures the security intent that the store value is exactly the register value, properly byte-aligned.

mappings.ns31a_signal: Load-data correctness: register gets exactly memory value
mappings.notes: Load-data correctness: when load response is valid, the output data (lsu_rdata_o) must be correctly sign/zero-extended from the memory data based on the load type and sign extension setting. This verifies the load data path integrity.

Address-computation correctness: when a load or store request is made, the address sent to memory (data_addr_o) must match the computed address from the ALU (adder_result_ex_i). This verifies that the effective address calculation is correct.
